_description: AC-3 / AC-6 parity fixture: the take-profit re-arm divergence on an MC-UNAVAILABLE tick mid-position. With TAKE_PROFIT_MC_PCT=5: tick 0 mc=2 tp-arms (above_tp_count=0); tick 1 mc=8 above-threshold (above_tp_count -> 1); tick 2 mc_prob=null is MC-UNAVAILABLE (run_monte_carlo's None sentinel) — production hits the `elif tp_armed` branch whose inner `else` RESETS above_tp_count to 0 because MC is absent; tick 3 mc=8 above-threshold again -> above_tp_count reaches only 1. Production: NO TP exit. The pre-fix replay substitutes 22.5 for the null tick (>= TAKE_PROFIT_MC_PCT), increments above_tp_count instead of resetting, and fires a PHANTOM Take-Profit exit on tick 2 (or tick 3). All ticks return > 0 so the return-sign disarm is not the variable. VWAP inert (VWAP_CROSS_HWM_PCT=99, valid_vwap_weight=0); vol low / returns flat so the trailing stop never fires.
_provenance: Hand-derived input sequence. Schema = synthetic_history tick dict; mc_prob=null encodes run_monte_carlo's MC_INSUFFICIENT_HISTORY_SENTINEL (None). NOTE re audit finding 9: a verified tick-by-tick trace showed production and the pre-fix replay behave IDENTICALLY on a sub-threshold MC DIP (mc back below TAKE_PROFIT_MC_PCT while available) — there is no divergence there. The real divergence is the MC-UNAVAILABLE case, coupled to AC-5. This fixture targets the real divergence. The parity test asserts production and the corrected replay both produce NO TP exit; pre-fix the replay diverges by firing one.
